tasks.title: Companies can create campaigns with AI assistance
tasks.description: DONE. Campaign wizard with product name, description, features, public links, AI generation, scraping, payout rates, max_users, min_engagement. Deployed and tested on Vercel.

tasks.title: AI matching connects campaigns to the right users
tasks.description: DONE. Hard filters (platforms, followers, engagement, region, max_users, 3-campaign limit). AI scoring via Gemini with fallback. Combined score formula. Code written and deployed.

tasks.title: Campaign detail page shows verified stats and analytics
tasks.description: DONE. Stats cards, per-platform breakdown, influencer table, invitation funnel, budget management. Clicks bug fixed. 19 unit tests passing.

tasks.title: Users can onboard: connect platforms, choose niches, set up API keys
tasks.description:
5-step onboarding flow.

Acceptance: complete onboarding  ->  profile scraped correctly  ->  niches saved  ->  API key tested  ->  agent starts.

INTEGRATION TEST (Slice 2):
1. Register new account  ->  login page works
2. Step 1: Connect all 4 platforms  ->  browser opens, user logs in, closes browser  ->  scraping starts automatically
3. Verify scraped data: each platform must have display_name, follower_count, following_count, bio, recent_posts (with engagement per post), engagement_rate > 0, profile_pic_url
4. Step 2: Niche checkboxes NOT pre-selected. AI suggestions shown as hint text only. Region auto-detected and shown read-only.
5. Step 3: Pick operating mode
6. Step 4: Paste Gemini API key  ->  click Test  ->  green check. At least 1 key required.
7. Step 5: Summary shows all data correctly  ->  click Start
8. Dashboard loads, background agent starts

BLOCKER: Scrapers return zero engagement/posts for X, Facebook, Reddit. LinkedIn gets followers but no engagement. UAT PASSED 8/8. BUG-001 fixed.

tasks.title: Content generation agent creates researched, platform-native UGC content
tasks.description:
Dedicated AI agent for UGC content creation.

Acceptance: accept a campaign  ->  agent generates distinct content per platform  ->  content feels like real UGC.

INTEGRATION TEST (Slice 2):
1. Go to Campaigns tab  ->  see Nike: Stay Fit invitation
2. Accept the campaign
3. Wait for background agent to generate content (runs every 2 min)
4. Go to campaign detail  ->  see today's drafts per platform (X, LinkedIn, Facebook, Reddit)
5. Each draft should be UGC-style, platform-native, genuinely different per platform
6. Drafts reference the product (Nike) and include must-include hashtags

DONE: 116 drafts generated across 3 campaigns. UGC style verified ' authentic per-platform content. Research phase working.

tasks.title: Users can review, edit, approve/reject daily content drafts
tasks.description:
Semi-auto review flow.

UAT PASSED 7/7: approve, reject, restore, edit all work. Approve All works after UTC fix. Post scheduled on approve. UTC/local time bug (BUG-01) fixed.

tasks.title: Posts are executed via Playwright and URLs are captured correctly
tasks.description:
Posting pipeline verification.

INTEGRATION TEST (Slice 2):
1. Approved drafts appear in post_schedule with scheduled times
2. Background agent picks up due posts (every 60s)
3. Playwright opens browser, posts content to platform
4. Post URL captured by navigating to own profile
5. local_post record created with correct post_url
6. Post synced to server (check /api/posts)
7. Desktop notification: 'Posted to [platform] for [campaign]'
8. Test on at least 1 platform (X or LinkedIn) end-to-end
9. IMPORTANT: Do NOT like or repost during pre/post browsing. Just browse and click around.

POSTING WORKS: LinkedIn post sent successfully (posted_no_url status). URL capture still fails but post is delivered. Retry logic added with 3 attempts + scroll. Graceful fallback creates local_post with placeholder URL. User should verify the post actually appeared on LinkedIn.

tasks.title: System tray icon and desktop notifications
tasks.description:
Desktop presence.

INTEGRATION TEST (Slice 2):
1. On app launch: tray icon appears in Windows taskbar
2. Right-click tray  ->  menu shows: Open Dashboard, Agent status, Pause/Resume Agent, Quit
3. Click Open Dashboard  ->  browser opens to localhost:5222
4. Close browser tab  ->  app keeps running (tray still visible)
5. Click Quit  ->  app fully exits
6. Desktop notifications appear for: new campaign invitations, drafts ready for review, post succeeded/failed

CODE DONE. UAT confirms tray icon is created (likely in Windows overflow area). Menu code correct. Desktop notifications wired. User should verify visually.

tasks.title: Metric scraping captures all data needed for billing
tasks.description:
Metric scraping verification.

INTEGRATION TEST (Slice 3):
1. After a post goes live, wait for T+1h scrape (or trigger manually)
2. Check local_metric table  ->  impressions, likes, reposts, comments captured
3. Compare scraped values to actual platform numbers
4. Metrics synced to server  ->  check /api/metrics
5. Billing triggered on submission  ->  check payout records created
6. Repeat for at least 2 platforms

BLOCKER: LinkedIn post didn't actually land (Playwright typed content but post didn't appear on profile). Need user to debug with headed browser. Metric scraping code is ready but needs a real post URL to test against.

tasks.title: Billing correctly calculates earnings from metrics

Unit tests cover: earnings formula, 20% platform cut, budget capping, CPM/CPE calculations, zero-division handling, payout rate suggestions.

INTEGRATION TEST (Slice 3):

2. Balance increases by charged amount

4. Delete draft campaign  ->  balance refunded
5. Payment history shows on billing page

tasks.title: Users can see earnings and request withdrawal

tasks.title: Fix bugs, edge cases, and labels

tasks.title: Explain: Company AI Wizard
tasks.description:
Walk through how the campaign AI wizard currently works. Fix issues found by user.

IMPLEMENTED:
1. IMAGE UPLOADS: Drag-and-drop upload to Supabase Storage, thumbnails preview, stored in Campaign.assets.image_urls
2. DEEP WEB CRAWLING: BFS crawler (httpx+BS4) follows same-domain links up to 10 pages, depth 2. Replaces shallow single-page scrape.
3. FILE UPLOADS: PDF/DOCX/TXT upload to Supabase Storage with text extraction (PyPDF2, python-docx). Stored in Campaign.assets.file_contents.
4. AI GENERATION FIX: Enhanced Gemini prompt uses all sources (deep-scraped content, uploaded files, images, product info). Fallback clearly marks [AI generation failed]. Error response now shows actual error.
5. BALANCE GATE: Companies with <$50 balance blocked from wizard.

KEY INSIGHT: The detailed campaign brief IS the single content source for amplifiers (users). It must contain everything needed to create posts: product details, scraped knowledge, file contents, images, must-include/avoid, content direction.

IMPACT AUDIT: After explaining, list which other features this touches. Format: IMPACTS: feature1 (what changes), feature2 (what changes). This helps track cascading changes across tasks.

tasks.title: Explain: AI Content Generation
tasks.description:
Walk through how AI content generation currently works. Identify gaps between current implementation and what it should be. The user will specify what to fix.

CURRENT STATE: Basic single-prompt content generation. No strategy phase, no competitor research, no goal-driven content.

WHAT IT SHOULD BE (user-specified):

AI AGENT: An actual AI agent should handle content generation through all 4 phases (research, strategy, creation, review). Not a single prompt call.

PHASE 1 - RESEARCH (weekly per campaign):
- Competitor research: what are competitors posting? What works for them?
- Trend research: what is trending in this niche right now?
- Product deep-dive: use ALL campaign sources (brief, scraped URLs, uploaded files, images)
- Research runs ONCE PER WEEK per active campaign, results cached and reused daily

PHASE 2 - STRATEGY (per campaign, refreshed weekly):
- Campaign goal drives content strategy:
  * LEADS: content has links to product, strong CTAs, landing page mentions
  * VIRALITY: emotionally triggering content, images/videos, high posting frequency to stay in peoples heads
  * BRAND AWARENESS: product mentions woven naturally, lifestyle content, consistent presence
  * ENGAGEMENT: questions, polls, controversial takes, discussion starters
- Post type per platform: text-only, image+text, or image-only (based on what performs best)
- Posting frequency: how many posts per day per platform
- Tone and angle: derived from research + campaign goal (NOT hardcoded)

PHASE 3 - CONTENT CREATION:
- Platform-native content that looks like REAL UGC (not polished marketing):
  * Hooks that stop the scroll (emotional triggers, curiosity gaps, contrarian takes)
  * Real-life scenarios and personal stories
  * Imperfect language (typos-level casual, not corporate-perfect)
  * Each platform has its own writing style (X: punchy + hashtags, LinkedIn: story format with line breaks, Reddit: genuine community member, Facebook: conversational)
- NOT compulsory to use all assets/hashtags in every draft
- Must-include items woven naturally where they fit, not forced
- Campaign images used where appropriate (not every post)
- Content types: text-only, image-only, image+text

PHASE 4 - REVIEW:
- Semi-auto: drafts shown to user with desktop notification
- Full-auto: auto-approved and scheduled

FREE API CREDITS: All content creation within free-tier limits. Gemini -> Mistral -> Groq fallback chain. Design to stay within daily free quotas.

IMPACTS: Task 25 (review must handle image+text drafts), Task 27 (posting must handle all content types), Task 39 (notifications per campaign), Task 52 (future image/video gen tools plug into Phase 3)

IMAGE INTELLIGENCE: When the company provides product images (Campaign.assets.image_urls), the AI must analyze each image (via vision API like Gemini Vision), understand what it shows (product shot, lifestyle photo, screenshot, infographic etc.), and decide WHICH posts should include WHICH images based on relevance. Not every post gets an image. The AI matches image content to post content â€” e.g., a product screenshot goes with a feature-focused post, a lifestyle photo goes with a story post. Images should NOT be blindly attached to every draft.

tasks.title: Verify: AI Content Generation
tasks.description:

SKIPPED: Current content gen is known to be inadequate (single-prompt, no strategy, no research). Requirements fully documented in task 23. Will be rebuilt as 4-phase AI agent (task 63) after all verification tasks complete.

tasks.title: Explain: Scheduled Posting
tasks.description:
Walk through how scheduled posting currently works ' post scheduling logic, time slot system, Playwright browser automation, platform-specific posting functions, URL capture, human behavior emulation, error handling. If the user finds it's not implemented the way they want, fix it.

CRITICAL: X (Twitter) locks user accounts when controlled by Playwright automation. Amplifier must detect when X has locked the account (e.g., by checking for lockout page/modal after login) and notify the user to log back in manually. This is a known blocker for X posting.

IMPACT AUDIT: After explaining, list which other features this touches. Format: IMPACTS: feature1 (what changes), feature2 (what changes). This helps track cascading changes across tasks.

CONTENT TYPES: Some content drafts may be image-only, text-only, or image+text. The posting pipeline must handle all 3 types correctly for each platform. For image-only posts, upload the image without text. For text-only, post without image. For image+text, upload both. Each platform has different upload flows for these cases.

BUGS FOUND DURING TESTING:
1. Reddit post URL broken (posted_no_url status) â€” URL capture failed, placeholder stored. User clicked the URL and got a 404 in the app.
2. Reddit post not actually appearing on Reddit â€” Playwright reported success but the post may not have landed (similar to LinkedIn issue from earlier sessions). Need to verify in headed mode.
3. Post URL links in the Posts page route to local app URLs instead of platform URLs â€” causes 404.

X LOCKOUT DETECTION (noted for later): When any activity opens X (posting, scraping), detect the locked account page (white page with heading like "Your account got locked") and notify user to unlock manually. Out of scope for current verify task.

The background agent already re-scrapes profiles weekly â€” but it does NOT send the updated data to the server. The server needs fresh profile data for AI matching to work well. Fix the background agent to sync scraped_profiles to server after every profile refresh. Consider increasing refresh frequency from weekly to daily.

tasks.title: Upgrade scrapers to use AI vision for robust profile extraction
tasks.description: Replace brittle CSS selector-based profile scraping with AI-powered extraction. Send screenshots or page text to Gemini Vision API to extract structured profile data. AI reads pages like a human  no selectors to break. Can also extract insights: content style, niche, audience, tone. See FUTURE.md for full spec.

tasks.title: Sophisticated AI content generation -- images, videos, memes for campaign goals
tasks.description:
Replace basic text-only content generation with rich media UGC. Content should be tailored to campaign goals (viral views, lead gen, brand awareness, FOMO, hype). Use ultra-cheap AI tools: FLUX.2 locally for images, LTX+TTS for videos, Supermeme.ai for memes, Canva/CapCut for polish. Train LoRAs per-user for face/style consistency. See FUTURE.md for full tool comparison and pipeline design.

CRITICAL: Do NOT lock in specific tools now. Re-research at implementation time. Build for where models will be in 3-6 months: cheaper, faster, running on phones. Architecture must be model-agnostic (pluggable providers). Prefer open-weight models that run locally over proprietary APIs.

ACTION ITEM: Add FLUX.1 as an image generation option for users. Cost: ~$0.30/hour on vast.ai. Give users this as an opt-in option for generating images to use in their posts. Ultra cheap and produces high-quality content. Ensure FLUX works smoothly for users end-to-end: generates content AND is posted to platforms successfully.

tasks.title: Update SLC spec to reflect verified functionality
tasks.description: After all explain/verify tasks (20-50) are complete, update SLC.md to accurately reflect what Amplifier does TODAY. This is the single living doc. Remove anything that doesn't exist, add anything that was changed during verify tasks. The result should be: if someone reads only this doc, they understand exactly what Amplifier does.

tasks.title: Flux.1 image generation for campaign posts (~$0.30/session)
tasks.description:
Add Flux.1 (vast.ai GPU rental) as an opt-in image generation option for users. ~$0.30 per session generates multiple high-quality UGC images per campaign.

What to build:
1. Integrate vast.ai API  spin up Flux.1 instance, send prompt, receive images, tear down
2. Add image gen step to content generation pipeline (after text gen)
3. Platform-specific formats: landscape (LinkedIn/Facebook), square (Instagram), vertical (TikTok)
4. Update draft model to store image file paths
5. Update posting pipeline to upload images (most platform functions already support this)
6. User settings: opt-in toggle for image gen
7. Campaign wizard: companies can request image content

Cost: user pays ~$0.30 per vast.ai GPU session. Amplifier does NOT subsidize this.

See FUTURE.md for full spec. Do NOT start until all verify tasks (20-50) are done.

tasks.title: Write tests for all verified features
tasks.description: After all explain/verify tasks are done and docs are updated, write tests to lock down the verified behavior. Cover: company wizard + AI generation, campaign matching, user onboarding + scraping, content generation pipeline, posting pipeline, metric scraping, billing calculation, earnings display, Stripe top-up, admin pages. Tests should test the actual behavior confirmed during verify tasks  not hypothetical behavior.

tasks.title: Integrate video generation (Seedance 2 or similar) as user-toggleable feature
tasks.description: Add video generation integration to Amplifier. Users can enable/disable from settings. Content pipeline produces short-form UGC videos for TikTok, Instagram Reels, LinkedIn, Facebook. Requirements: 1) Research best ultra-cheap video gen tool at implementation time 2) Plug-and-play toggle in user settings 3) Enable/disable per platform 4) Videos stored locally, attached to drafts 5) Campaign wizard: companies can request video content 6) Model-agnostic architecture. Do NOT lock in a specific tool now.

tasks.title: Use official social media APIs for profile data (X, LinkedIn, Reddit, Facebook)
tasks.description: Replace/supplement Playwright scraping with official platform APIs for richer, more reliable profile data. See FUTURE.md.

tasks.title: AI campaign quality gate (85% minimum before activation)
tasks.description: AI checks campaign completeness and quality before company can activate. Below 85% gets specific feedback. Needs further discussion on whether to block or warn. See FUTURE.md.

tasks.title: AI browser agent for profile scraping (browser-use or similar)
tasks.description: Replace fixed CSS selector scraping with AI browser agents that navigate profiles like humans. More robust, deeper insights, better anti-detection. Evaluate browser-use, AgentQL, Skyvern. See FUTURE.md.

tasks.title: Ensure metrics accuracy for billing (critical for trust)
tasks.description: Metrics must be accurate â€” earnings depend on them. Cross-validate, handle edge cases, consider APIs over scraping, add sanity checks and audit trails. See FUTURE.md.

tasks.title: Self-learning content generation (learn from performance + trends)
tasks.description: AI learns from: 1) what competitors/industry post, 2) own post performance (which hooks/formats worked), 3) social/cultural trends. Experiments with styles, doubles down on winners. See FUTURE.md.

tasks.title: Free and paid tiers for amplifiers (users) and companies
tasks.description: Design and implement tiered pricing. Free tier: limited campaigns, basic content gen, standard features. Paid tier: unlimited campaigns, AI image/video gen, priority matching, advanced analytics. Free tier must be genuinely useful. See FUTURE.md for full spec.

tasks.title: Build 4-phase AI content agent (research, strategy, creation, review)
tasks.description:
Replace single-prompt content generator with a sophisticated 4-phase AI agent. See FUTURE.md for full architecture.

Phases: 1) Weekly research (product, competitors, trends, image analysis via vision API), 2) Goal-driven strategy (leads vs virality vs awareness vs engagement), 3) Daily platform-native UGC creation (hooks, stories, real language, image matching), 4) Review flow (semi-auto or full-auto).

BEFORE BUILDING: Research existing AI tools/frameworks that could handle this. Evaluate: Jasper API, Copy.ai API, CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph, FeedHive API, Predis.ai. Goal is leverage â€” use an existing tool if it does 80% of what we need. Only build custom if nothing fits.

If building custom: use Gemini/Mistral/Groq APIs with the 4-phase ContentAgent architecture (see FUTURE.md for class diagram).

Do NOT start until all verification tasks (25-50) are done.

SCHEDULING: The content agent (Phase 2 Strategy) should determine the scheduled posting time for each draft, not the review/approval flow. The agent decides WHEN to post based on campaign goal, target region timezone, platform peak hours, and posting frequency strategy.

tasks.title: Upgrade posting system for all content formats across 6 platforms (incl. TikTok + Instagram)
tasks.description: Update post.py to handle all common content formats per platform: X (threads, multi-image, polls), LinkedIn (carousels/PDFs, polls, articles, video), Facebook (albums, polls, video, stories), Reddit (link posts, image posts, polls, cross-posting), Instagram (carousels, Reels, Stories), TikTok (video, slideshows, duets). Re-enable Instagram and TikTok. Content generator must produce format-specific drafts. See FUTURE.md for full format list per platform.

tasks.title: Platform-specific content preview in review UI
tasks.description: Show draft content as a visual mockup of how it would appear on each platform (X tweet card, LinkedIn post card, Reddit post, etc.) instead of plain text. This is a UI improvement, not functional â€” drafts already work as plain text.

tasks.title: Detect X (Twitter) account lockout and notify user to unlock
tasks.description: When Amplifier opens X for posting or scraping, detect if the account is locked. X shows a minimal white page with heading like "Your account got locked" when automation is detected. Amplifier must: 1) Check for lockout page after navigating to X, 2) If detected, skip posting/scraping for X, 3) Send desktop notification telling user to manually unlock their X account, 4) Mark X session as "locked" in session health so it stops retrying until user re-authenticates.

tasks.title: Improve session health check reliability
tasks.description: Current session_health.py returns yellow (uncertain) for platforms that are actually logged in (LinkedIn, Reddit, X). Auth element selectors need updating to match current platform UIs. Reduce false-positive expired notifications. Consider: retry logic before notifying, use actual posting success as a health signal instead of element detection, suppress notifications if posts are succeeding on that platform.

tasks.title: Repost campaign type â€” companies provide ready-made posts for users to repost
tasks.description:
Add a new campaign type: repost campaigns. Instead of AI-generating content, companies provide pre-written posts per platform (X, LinkedIn, Facebook, Reddit) as part of the campaign. Users accept the campaign and Amplifier reposts these exact posts on a schedule to the user's social media profiles.

Company side:
- New campaign type selector: "AI Generated" (existing) vs "Repost" (new)
- For repost campaigns, company provides per-platform post content (text, images, links) directly in the campaign creation form
- Each campaign can have multiple posts (a content calendar the company defines)
- Company can specify posting order, frequency, and date range

User side:
- Repost campaigns show the actual posts in the invitation (so users know what they're agreeing to post)
- On accept, posts are scheduled automatically â€” no content generation step, no review step needed
- User can optionally preview before each post goes live (semi_auto mode) or let them auto-post (full_auto)
- Posts execute via existing Playwright posting engine (post.py)
- Metrics scraped and billed the same as AI-generated campaigns

Server side:
- New campaign_type field on Campaign model ("ai_generated" or "repost")
- New campaign_posts table: campaign_id, platform, content, image_url, post_order, scheduled_offset
- Matching works the same â€” repost campaigns still matched to users by niche/platform/followers
- Billing works the same â€” pay per impression/engagement

This is simpler than AI campaigns (no content generation, no research, no strategy) and gives companies more control over exact messaging.

tasks.title: Update all docs to match verified codebase
tasks.description: Run the docs-updater agent to sync all 16 docs in docs/ with the actual codebase after all explain/verify tasks (#15-#50) are complete. Do NOT run this until all verify tasks are done â€” code changes during verify will invalidate doc updates. Single pass: read current code, update every doc, commit.

tasks.title: Fix draft notification count â€” shows stale drafts from all days
tasks.description: Two bugs in the pending drafts notification: (1) get_pending_drafts() counts ALL unapproved drafts across all dates, not just today. Stale drafts from days ago inflate the count (showed 54 when only 12 were from today). Fix: filter to today only, or at minimum only count drafts from the last 24h. (2) On first page load, _lastPendingCount in user.js starts at 0, so the first /api/status poll always fires a notification (pending - 0 = pending). Fix: initialize _lastPendingCount to -1 or fetch initial count before enabling notifications. Files: scripts/utils/local_db.py (get_pending_drafts), scripts/user_app.py (/api/status), scripts/static/js/user.js (pollStatus).

tasks.title: User App: Password reset flow
tasks.description: Add email-based password reset with time-limited tokens. No reset mechanism exists — users locked out permanently if they forget password. Add /api/auth/forgot-password and /api/auth/reset-password endpoints. Send reset link via email (or display token for MVP).

tasks.title: User App: CSRF protection on all Flask forms
tasks.description: Add CSRF tokens to all POST forms in user_app.py. Install flask-wtf or implement manual CSRF tokens. Currently no CSRF protection exists — POST endpoints vulnerable to cross-site request forgery.

tasks.title: User App: Encrypt stored credentials
tasks.description: Auth tokens in server_auth.json and API keys in local SQLite are plaintext. Use OS keychain (keyring lib) or AES encryption at rest. If device is compromised, all tokens and keys are exposed.

tasks.title: User App: Rate limiting + API key validation + campaign search
tasks.description: Three items: (1) Add rate limiting on login/register (5 attempts/min). (2) Test API key validity on save in settings — call the provider and verify before accepting. (3) Add search by title and filter by status to campaign list on dashboard.

tasks.title: User App: Improve content draft UX
tasks.description: Five items: (1) Show campaign content_guidance alongside draft during review. (2) Add draft versioning — store original AI-generated + user-edited versions. (3) Add character counts for all platforms (LinkedIn 3000, Facebook 63206, Reddit 40000). (4) Fix Reddit JSON format error handling with try/except. (5) Add "preview exactly what will be posted" step before posting.

tasks.title: User App: Fix invitation UX gaps
tasks.description: Four items: (1) Add expiry countdown timers ("expires in 2h 15m") instead of raw timestamps. (2) Clearly mark expired invitations with EXPIRED badge and gray them out. (3) Capture optional decline reason when user rejects campaigns — send to company. (4) Require at least 1 niche during onboarding for campaign matching to work.

tasks.title: User App: Data integrity improvements
tasks.description: Medium priority. (1) Periodic local DB backup to a second file. (2) Sync approved drafts to server so laptop crash doesn't lose them. (3) Wrap SQLite operations in proper transactions. (4) Check campaign_version on detail view to detect stale cached data.

tasks.title: User App: Settings, metrics, and performance improvements
tasks.description: Medium priority. Settings: auto-populate follower counts from scraped data, per-platform auto/manual mode, sync settings with server on startup. Metrics: manual metric entry fallback, resilient CSS selectors, 3-day profile refresh. Performance: campaign pagination, API response caching (5min TTL), limit concurrent Playwright to 2-3, parallel image generation.

tasks.title: User App: UX polish and integration fixes
tasks.description: Medium priority. UX: rename confusing statuses (pending_invitation→Invited, content_generated→Draft Ready), copy-to-clipboard for post URLs, mobile responsive, client-side form validation, CSV export for earnings. Integration: server-side post URL dedup, client-side invitation expiry warnings, campaign conflict detection.

tasks.title: User App: Compliance, accessibility, and testing
tasks.description: Low priority. Compliance: ToS acceptance on registration, privacy policy, GDPR data export/deletion, account deletion. Accessibility: ARIA labels, WCAG color contrast, keyboard navigation, form label associations. Testing: E2E tests (full flow), unit tests (scraping, generation, scheduling), mock server fixtures, load testing.
